classification: Supplier-named organ-targeted short regulatory peptide family

mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM

Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: CONFLICTING SUPPLIER IDENTITY / IMMUNE BIOREGULATOR NAME
closure_state: BLOCKED — PUBLIC SOURCES CONFLICT BETWEEN EDP AND PQN IDENTITIES, NO AUTHORITATIVE PRIMARY SEQUENCE RECORD WAS CLOSED, AND CRY20 TERMINI, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND HUMAN EVIDENCE REMAIN OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: Crystagen is marketed as an immune-system short peptide, but accessible public sources conflict on its molecular identity, including Glu-Asp-Pro (EDP) and Pro-Gln-Asn (PQN). No authoritative primary-sequence record or curated chemical database entry was sufficient to choose one identity. CertiAura therefore retains Crystagen as an identity-conflicted supplier family.
critical_closure_controls: Do not assign EDP without direct primary and analytical closure
Do not assign PQN without direct primary and analytical closure
No Thymalin, Vilon, Thymogen or other immune-peptide evidence transfer
No immune-restoration, infection-prevention or anti-ageing claim
No dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
